sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Real Valladolid hosts Girona FC at the José Zorrilla Stadium in Valladolid for an intriguing La Liga clash. Valladolid, sitting at the bottom of the table in 20th place with just 16 points, are desperate for a turnaround. Girona FC, positioned at 16th with 38 points, are in a slightly better spot but still need points to climb away from the relegation zone. This matchup promises intensity as both teams have much to prove. The referee, Alejandro Quintero Gonzalez, averages 5.2 yellow cards per game and 0.28 red cards per game, suggesting a potentially feisty encounter.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these sides, Girona FC holds the upper hand with 3 wins compared to Valladolid's single victory and 1 draw. Their most recent meeting on December 20, 2024, saw Girona dominate with a 3-0 victory at home, where Miguel Gutiérrez earned the Player of the Match title with an impressive 8.9 Sofascore rating. In head-to-head trends, Girona has been the first to score in 4 out of the last 5 games, giving them a psychological edge. Notably, matches between these teams have often seen less than 4.5 cards in all of the last 5 encounters, hinting at relatively disciplined play.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Real Valladolid are in dreadful form, with a streak of 9 consecutive losses and no wins in their last 16 matches. They’ve also failed to keep a clean sheet in 16 games and conceded first in many recent outings, while their games frequently see over 2.5 goals, happening in 7 of their last 7 matches. Girona FC, on the other hand, have struggled defensively on the road, conceding first in 8 of their last 10 away games. However, both teams tend to keep corner counts low, with Valladolid under 10.5 corners in 7 of their last 9 games and Girona in 6 of their last 7.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Real Valladolid, forward Mamadou Sylla stands out with 5 goals this season and a standout record against Girona with 2 goals and 2 assists in past matchups, averaging a 7.24 Sofascore rating over 2 starts. Raúl Moro, another forward, is in form with 1 goal and 3 assists in his last 5 matches, boasting a 6.88 average rating. Darwin Machís and Florian Grillitsch are notable for their aerial prowess, both ranking 4th in the league with 75% of aerial duels won. On Girona’s side, Cristhian Stuani leads with 9 goals and 2 assists, showing clinical finishing with a 1.9034 xG overperformance. Viktor Tsygankov, with 2 goals and 4 assists, and Daley Blind, averaging a strong 7.06 rating, will also be crucial.

sections.subtitle: Missing Players Impact
sections.text: Real Valladolid will miss key defender Javi Sánchez, who has 2 goals and a 6.73 average rating, potentially weakening their backline. Girona FC faces a bigger challenge with several absences, including Yangel Herrera, who ranks 3rd in the league for fouls drawn per game at 2.4 and has 4 goals and 3 assists. Bryan Gil and Miguel Gutiérrez, the latter with 5 assists and a 7.03 rating, are also out, which could dent Girona’s attacking fluency and defensive stability on the left flank.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Statistics
sections.text: Valladolid’s season stats reveal their struggles, with only 26 goals scored (league rank 20th) and a staggering 85 goals conceded (worst in the league). Despite this, they rank 2nd in goals vs expected goals overperformance with a +5.9853 difference, showing some efficiency in attack. They excel in ground duels, ranking 4th with 51.73% won, and interceptions per game at 8.26 (also 4th). Girona’s goal distribution shows they peak late, with 26.42% of their goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes. Their ability to score in the 31-45 minute period is also strong, with 28.3% of goals in that window, which could test Valladolid’s shaky defense early in halves.

sections.subtitle: Interesting Facts
sections.text: Real Valladolid’s home games have seen both teams scoring in their last 5 matches, indicating a pattern of defensive vulnerability but attacking intent. Girona’s Cristhian Stuani has outperformed his expected goals by 1.9034, making him one of the most clinical strikers in the league. Valladolid’s average ball possession of 42.23% ranks 17th, often forcing them into a defensive setup, while their 4.03 corners per game (15th in league) suggest limited set-piece opportunities. Girona’s last standout player against Valladolid, Miguel Gutiérrez, won’t feature due to absence, potentially shifting the dynamic of their left-sided attacks.
